# The spread of low-credibility content by social bots

**Authors:** Chengcheng Shao et al.  
**Year:** 2018  
**URL:** https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-018-06930-7  
**Evidence type:** Large-scale observational network analysis

## Claim supported

Bots can play a disproportionate role in early amplification and humans reshare bot-posted content.

## Key result

Analysis of 14 million messages and 400,000 articles found bots amplified low-credibility content early before virality.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
